name: Advanced UX & Features
description: Real-time WebSocket, reactions, search, dark mode, AI, analytics, A/B testing

sharedRules.noEmojis: No emojis in code, comments, UI, logs, or documentation
sharedRules.featureBasedModules: Organize code by feature, not by layer
sharedRules.researchFirst: Use Context7 MCP + Brave Search before ANY implementation. Document findings.
sharedRules.workflowEnforced: Commit → Push → Update JSON → Write Report. In that order.
sharedRules.testBeforeComplete: Run full test suite before marking step complete. All tests must pass.
sharedRules.fullyImplemented: No placeholders, TODOs, or incomplete code. Everything must work.
sharedRules.modularArchitecture: Keep files under 250 lines. Split when needed.
sharedRules.seoOptimized: All frontend pages must have proper meta tags, structured data, and semantic HTML
sharedRules.apiVersioning: All APIs must be versioned (v1, v2) with backward compatibility policy

workflow: 1. Research: Use Context7 MCP (library docs) + Brave Search (best practices)
5. Run all tests: npm run test:all (or equivalent)
7. Commit with conventional commit: feat(phase{}): step Y - title
9. Update this phase{}.json with completion data
11. Mark step as completed in JSON
12. Create Pull Request, get review, merge

steps.title: Research Real-time WebSocket Architecture Patterns
steps.objective: Complete implementation of Research Real-time WebSocket Architecture Patterns with production-ready quality, following all mandatory rules.

requiredSearches: Research Real-time WebSocket Architecture Patterns implementation examples
questions: What are the security considerations for ['research', 'real-time']?
How to test ['research', 'real-time'] effectively?
What are common pitfalls in ['research', 'real-time'] implementation?

step1.description: Design and implement the core Research Real-time WebSocket Architecture Patterns system
details: Follow shared rules: no emojis, max 250 lines/file, primary colors only
Write unit tests first (TDD approach recommended)
Ensure all code is type-safe with TypeScript
Add proper error handling and logging
Document API with OpenAPI spec if applicable

step2.description: Complete Research Real-time WebSocket Architecture Patterns implementation with validation and error handling
details: Implement full CRUD operations if applicable
Add input validation with Zod schemas
Write integration tests
Add proper HTTP status codes and error messages

step3.description: Test and validate Research Real-time WebSocket Architecture Patterns thoroughly
details: Run all tests: npm run test:all
Check code coverage > 90% for critical paths
Perform manual testing of all scenarios
Verify no console errors or warnings
Test security implications

tests: Integration tests for critical paths pass
manuallyVerify: Feature works as expected in development environment
Security controls are effective (RLS, rate limiting, etc.)
UI follows design system (primary colors only)
acceptanceCriteria: Complete implementation of Research Real-time WebSocket Architecture Patterns

deliverables: Updated OpenAPI spec if API changes

reportTemplate.summary: Brief summary of what was implemented for Research Real-time WebSocket Architecture Patterns
